name: make-plan
tagline: Orchestrates a phased implementation-plan skill that delegates doc discovery to subagents before writing plan phases.
verdict: Clean, low-risk process skill. It only instructs the agent on how to structure planning work (docs discovery, phased plan, verification checklist) — no network calls, no credential access, no shell execution, no hidden instructions. Minor gaps: no negative-trigger guidance, no version/changelog field, and no explicit tool declaration in frontmatter, so a use

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance (e.g., when a plan isn't needed, or for trivial one-line fixes) is given anywhere in description or body.
No explicit tool declaration (e.g., allowed-tools frontmatter field) despite the skill relying on subagent/Task-style delegation, grep, and test execution described only in prose.
No version field, changelog, or last-updated marker anywhere in the file, so consumers can't detect changes between installs.
